assertions.description: Error.isError is a function (the proposal adds a static method to Error).
Error.isError detects subclassed builtins (TypeError, RangeError, etc.).
assertions.test: Error.isError(new TypeError('x')) === true && Error.isError(new RangeError('y')) === true

assertions.description: A plain object that looks like an error ({ message, stack }) is not an error.
Primitives and other built-ins return false.
assertions.test: Error.isError(null) === false && Error.isError('a string') === false && Error.isError([]) === false

assertions.description: Error.isError detects Error instances from a different realm (e.g. an <iframe>'s window).
